arxiv.title: Neural-Symbolic Reasoning over Knowledge Graphs: A Survey from a Query Perspective
arxiv.abstract: Knowledge graph reasoning is pivotal in various domains such as data mining, artificial intelligence, the Web, and social sciences. arxiv.title: Opus: A Large Work Model for Complex Workflow Generation
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces Opus, a novel framework for generating and optimizing Workflows tailored to complex Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) use cases, focusing on cost reduction and quality enhancement while adhering to established industry processes and operational constraints. arxiv.title: The Well: a Large-Scale Collection of Diverse Physics Simulations for Machine Learning
arxiv.abstract: Machine learning based surrogate models offer researchers powerful tools for accelerating simulation-based workflows. arxiv.title: Polish-English medical knowledge transfer: A new benchmark and results
arxiv.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant potential in handling specialized tasks, including medical problem-solving. arxiv.title: Accelerating Multimodal Large Language Models by Searching Optimal Vision Token Reduction
arxiv.abstract: Prevailing Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) encode the input image(s) as vision tokens and feed them into the language backbone, similar to how Large Language Models (LLMs) process the text tokens. arxiv.title: Unveiling Performance Challenges of Large Language Models in Low-Resource Healthcare: A Demographic Fairness Perspective
arxiv.abstract: This paper studies the performance of large language models (LLMs), particularly regarding demographic fairness, in solving real-world healthcare tasks. arxiv.title: Rank It, Then Ask It: Input Reranking for Maximizing the Performance of LLMs on Symmetric Tasks
arxiv.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have quickly emerged as practical and versatile tools that provide new solutions for a wide range of domains.
